rationale: §8a Abs.1 BSIG requires KRITIS operators to protect availability, integrity, authenticity and confidentiality of IT systems; ISO 22301 §4.1 requires understanding the organizational context including legal/regulatory obligations, establishing the foundation for the BCMS.

§8a Abs.1 obligation to protect critical-service availability for the public mirrors ISO 22301 §4.2 requirement to understand interested parties (regulators, the public, sector-authority) and their needs/expectations.

§8a Abs.1 requires state-of-the-art risk-proportionate measures; ISO 22301 §6.1 requires the organization to identify risks and opportunities that could affect the BCMS, which is the planning-layer precondition for availability assurance.

BSI KdA control group ISMS requires top-management commitment and a security policy for the KRITIS scope; ISO 22301 §5.1 requires demonstrable leadership and commitment to the BCMS with the same purpose.

BSI KdA ISMS requirements include a documented information security policy aligned to §8a obligations; ISO 22301 §5.2 requires a BCMS policy that is appropriate to the organization's purpose and includes commitment to continual improvement.

BSI KdA ISMS requires defined roles, responsibilities and authorities for security management of the KRITIS scope; ISO 22301 §5.3 requires assignment of responsibilities and authorities for BCMS roles — the scope and intent are equivalent.

§8a Abs.1 and BSI KdA risk management controls require a documented risk identification and treatment process proportionate to critical-service risk; ISO 22301 §6.1 mandates the same — identifying risks/opportunities relevant to the BCMS.

BSI KdA risk management controls require systematic identification, assessment, and treatment of threats to KRITIS IT systems; ISO 22301 §8.2.3 requires the organization to conduct a risk assessment covering threats, vulnerabilities, and impacts on continuity.

§8a Abs.1 requires organizational and technical measures to ensure availability of critical services; ISO 22301 §8.2.1 requires establishing a formal documented BIA/RA process, the mandatory first step for continuity planning.

BSI KdA BCM controls require identification of critical activities, RTO/RPO and minimum service levels for the critical service; ISO 22301 §8.2.2 requires a Business Impact Analysis specifying prioritized activities, MTPD, RTO, RPO, and minimum resource levels.

§8a Abs.1 availability assurance requires identifying and selecting strategies to prevent/reduce disruptions to critical services; ISO 22301 §8.3.1 requires identifying continuity strategies addressing pre-, during-, and post-disruption phases.

BSI KdA BCM requires documented solutions (redundancies, alternative sites, manual procedures) to ensure critical-service availability; ISO 22301 §8.3.2 requires identifying and selecting strategies and solutions for continuity based on BIA outputs.

BSI KdA BCM requires identification and allocation of all resources (personnel, technology, facilities) needed to maintain critical services; ISO 22301 §8.3.3 requires determining resource requirements for each strategy and solution.

§8a Abs.1 requires documented emergency plans for continuity of critical services; ISO 22301 §8.4.1 (General) requires establishing, implementing, and maintaining plans and procedures to manage disruptions.

BSI KdA BCM requires a crisis response structure with defined roles, escalation paths, and decision-making authority; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 requires a documented response structure covering incident management, crisis management and business continuity.

BSI KdA BCM requires warning and alert procedures to notify relevant internal and external parties when a disruption occurs; ISO 22301 §8.4.3 requires warning and communication procedures covering internal notification, external communications, and regulatory reporting.

§8a Abs.1 and BSI KdA BCM require documented, tested, and regularly updated IT emergency/continuity plans; ISO 22301 §8.4.4 requires business continuity plans documenting response actions, roles, contacts, and recovery procedures.

BSI KdA BCM requires documented recovery procedures to restore critical services within defined timeframes; ISO 22301 §8.4.5 requires recovery plans and procedures to restore normal operations within RTO.

BSI KdA BCM requires regular exercises and tests to validate the effectiveness of emergency plans for critical services; ISO 22301 §8.5 requires an exercise program to verify BCMS effectiveness through simulated disruptions at planned intervals.

BSI KdA personal security controls require background checks and defined security roles for employees with KRITIS system access; ISO 22301 §7.2 (Competence) requires identifying and ensuring competence of personnel performing BCMS-relevant roles.

BSI KdA personnel security requires awareness training on security obligations for KRITIS IT systems; ISO 22301 §7.3 (Awareness) requires all personnel to be aware of the BCMS policy, their contribution, and implications of non-conformance.

BSI KdA requires key personnel roles to be defined for crisis response; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 requires defined response structure roles including crisis management team membership — overlap on personnel role assignment for crisis scenarios.

BSI KdA physical security controls require protection of KRITIS facilities against physical threats; ISO 22301 §8.3.2 requires identifying solutions that include physical infrastructure protection as part of the BC strategy — physical security is a sub-element.

BSI KdA requires physical resource inventory (buildings, UPS, fire suppression) to maintain critical-service availability; ISO 22301 §8.3.3 requires identifying resource requirements including facilities and physical infrastructure for continuity strategies.

BSI KdA network security controls require segmentation, firewalls, and redundant network paths to protect critical-service availability; ISO 22301 §8.3.2 requires selecting solutions including network redundancy as a BC solution to prevent and reduce disruption duration.

BSI KdA network incident response requires documented procedures for network failover and recovery; ISO 22301 §8.4.4 requires BC plans to include procedures for managing technology disruptions including network failures.

BSI KdA logging controls require central, tamper-proof audit logs for KRITIS systems to support incident detection and forensics; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 response structure requires documented evidence of incident timeline and decisions during a disruption event.

BSI KdA logging requirements provide monitoring data for measuring the effectiveness of security measures; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires monitoring, measurement, analysis, and evaluation of BCMS performance using defined metrics.

BSI KdA detection controls require threat detection mechanisms to trigger timely incident response; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 requires a response structure that activates upon detection of a disruption — detection is the trigger mechanism for BCMS activation.

BSI KdA detection must trigger warning and alerting procedures to relevant personnel and authorities; ISO 22301 §8.4.3 requires warning and communication procedures that activate once a disruption or threat is detected.

BSI KdA continuous monitoring/detection provides observability data used to measure KRITIS security posture; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires measurement and analysis of BCMS effectiveness through KPIs and monitoring indicators.

BSI KdA supply-chain security requires identification of critical suppliers and dependencies that could affect critical-service continuity; ISO 22301 §8.2.2 BIA requires identification of dependencies including third-party services in the impact analysis.

BSI KdA supply-chain controls require alternative suppliers and contractual continuity provisions; ISO 22301 §8.3.2 requires selecting solutions that address supply-chain dependencies as part of the continuity strategy.

BSI KdA requires emergency plans covering supplier failure scenarios; ISO 22301 §8.4.4 requires BC plans to address scenarios including loss of critical suppliers and document alternative sourcing procedures.

§8a Abs.1a (since 01.05.2023) requires deployment of attack-detection systems (SzA) as an operational measure; ISO 22301 §8.1 operational planning and control requires establishing all operational processes needed to meet BCMS objectives, of which detection is a precondition.

§8a Abs.1a SzA requires continuous automated parameter capture and log evaluation; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires monitoring and measurement of BCMS-relevant parameters — attack-detection logs contribute to security performance measurement.

§8a Abs.1a SzA requires automated detection of attack patterns to trigger incident response; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 response structure requires defined triggers and escalation mechanisms to activate the crisis management team — SzA detection is a sub-enabler.

§8a Abs.1a SzA must generate alerts that trigger notification to the relevant response parties; ISO 22301 §8.4.3 warning and communication procedures require automatic or manual alerting upon detection of a disruption — SzA alert output feeds this.

§8a Abs.1a SzA requires remediation measures for detected threats; ISO 22301 §8.4.4 BC plans must include actions to manage and resolve disruptions, including cyber-attack scenarios — SzA response playbooks map into plan procedures.

§8a Abs.1a mandates a minimum SzA maturity level (stage 3/Reifegrad 3) verified at audit; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires measurement and evaluation of security/BCMS performance — maturity measurement overlaps but SzA maturity is specific to cybersecurity rather than broader BCMS performance.

§8a Abs.3 requires KRITIS operators to provide proof of compliance every two years via audit, inspection, or recognized certification; ISO 22301 §9.2 requires a planned internal audit program to assess BCMS conformance — both require systematic, evidence-based periodic assurance.

§8a Abs.3 audit results including identified deficiencies must be reported and addressed; ISO 22301 §9.3 requires management review of BCMS performance including audit results, with decisions on improvement — equivalent obligation to review and act on audit outputs.

§8a Abs.3 audit scope is defined by the KRITIS facility's critical-service boundary (Geltungsbereich); ISO 22301 §4.3 requires the organization to define and document the BCMS scope including the boundaries — both mandate a defined, documented scope for assurance purposes.

§8a Abs.3 requires that identified deficiencies from KRITIS audits are communicated to BSI and must be remediated; ISO 22301 §10.1 requires nonconformities to be documented, root-cause analyzed, corrected, and eliminated — equivalent corrective action obligation.

§8a Abs.3 implies continual improvement through repeated two-year audit cycles; ISO 22301 §10.2 requires continual improvement of the suitability, adequacy, and effectiveness of the BCMS — structurally equivalent improvement obligation.

§8a Abs.3 requires operators to provide documented proof (B3S certification, audit reports, ISO 22301 cert) to the BSI; ISO 22301 §7.5 requires documented information to be created, controlled, and maintained as evidence of BCMS conformance.

§8b Abs.4 requires KRITIS operators to report significant IT security incidents to BSI within 24 hours of awareness; ISO 22301 §8.4.3 requires documented warning and communication procedures including timely notification to external authorities — the 24-hour notification is an instance of this requirement.

§8b Abs.4 initial incident report requires identification of affected systems, likely cause, and initial impact; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 response structure requires establishing an incident management process with initial assessment and documentation — §8b reporting is a regulatory output of this process.

§8b Abs.4 intermediate report (within ongoing incident management) requires updates on affected systems and containment measures; ISO 22301 §8.4.4 BC plans must include progress tracking procedures during active disruptions — intermediate reporting is a plan element.

§8b Abs.4 final report requires documentation of root cause, impact, and measures taken; ISO 22301 §8.4.5 recovery procedures require post-incident documentation of restoration actions — the final regulatory report draws from BCMS recovery documentation.

§8b Abs.4 final incident report includes lessons learned and corrective measures; ISO 22301 §10.1 requires root-cause analysis and corrective action for nonconformities — the regulatory post-incident review feeds the BCMS improvement cycle.

§8b Abs.4 reporting threshold (significant impairment of critical-service functionality) aligns with ISO 22301 §8.2.2 BIA outputs — the threshold is defined by the minimum service level (MBCO) and MTPD determined in the BIA.

§8b Abs.4 threshold requires operators to have defined criteria for declaring a significant incident; ISO 22301 §8.4.2 response structure requires decision criteria and escalation thresholds for activating incident and crisis management — threshold definition is a component of the response structure.

§8b Abs.3 requires KRITIS operators to register a contact point with BSI for incident reporting; ISO 22301 §7.4 (Communication) requires defining who communicates, when, and to which external parties including regulators — BSI contact registration is an instance of external communication planning.

§8b contact registration ensures the regulatory notification channel is pre-established; ISO 22301 §8.4.3 requires contact lists for external parties including regulatory bodies to be maintained as part of the warning and communication procedure.

BSI-KritisV sector thresholds (e.g., 500,000 persons served for water, 3,700 GWh/a for energy) determine which facilities are KRITIS-regulated; ISO 22301 §4.3 requires defining the BCMS scope based on the organization's critical activities and regulatory obligations — KritisV thresholds drive the BCMS scope boundary.

BSI-KritisV thresholds define the critical services whose availability must be protected; ISO 22301 §8.2.2 BIA identifies and prioritizes activities that are critical to service delivery — the KritisV-defined critical service is the primary subject of the BIA.

§8a Abs.1 establishes availability of the critical service as the primary protection objective; ISO 22301 §6.2 requires BCMS objectives to be established including specific availability and recovery targets — the KRITIS availability obligation translates directly to BCMS objectives.

§8a Abs.1 availability objective covers IT systems, components, and processes; ISO 22301 §8.2.2 BIA covers critical activities across all resources — ISO 22301 BIA is broader (includes non-IT assets like personnel, facilities) while KRITIS focuses on IT systems, making the standard a superset on scope.

§8a Abs.1 'state of the art' requirement mandates ongoing review of measures against current threat landscape; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires periodic monitoring and evaluation of BCMS effectiveness, ensuring measures remain adequate over time.

§8a Abs.1 'state of the art' mandate requires adaptation of measures as technology and threats evolve; ISO 22301 §10.2 continual improvement requires the BCMS to be updated to reflect changing context, threats, and lessons learned.

§8a Abs.1 proportionality principle requires measures to be commensurate with the disruption risk; ISO 22301 §6.1 requires risk-based planning where actions address risks proportionate to their potential impact on BCMS objectives.

BSI KdA BCM controls require training and qualification of personnel responsible for executing emergency plans; ISO 22301 §7.2 requires ensuring competence of personnel performing BCMS-relevant tasks through education, training, and experience.

BSI KdA BCM requires all relevant personnel to understand their roles in crisis and emergency scenarios; ISO 22301 §7.3 awareness requires personnel to understand the BCMS policy, their contribution, and consequences of non-compliance.

BSI KdA BCM requires regular exercises (tabletop and live) to test emergency plans for critical services; ISO 22301 §8.5 requires an exercise program including objectives, schedule, types of exercises, and post-exercise review — directly equivalent obligations.

BSI KdA BCM requires post-exercise review and update of emergency plans based on exercise results; ISO 22301 §8.5 requires exercises to result in evaluation reports with identified improvements — post-exercise plan update is a sub-requirement of §8.5.

BSI KdA BCM post-exercise gaps identified must be remediated; ISO 22301 §10.1 requires corrective actions for nonconformities — exercise-identified gaps are a form of nonconformity triggering corrective action.

BSI KdA supply-chain controls require contractual security and continuity obligations from critical suppliers; ISO 22301 §8.1 operational planning and control requires that outsourced processes relevant to the BCMS are controlled — supplier contracts are an operational control.

BSI KdA supply-chain security requires ongoing monitoring of supplier security performance; ISO 22301 §9.1 requires monitoring and measurement of performance including outsourced processes contributing to critical services.

§8a Abs.3 requires audit results and deficiencies to be submitted to BSI; ISO 22301 §9.3 management review requires review of BCMS performance with outputs including decisions and improvement actions — BSI submission is a regulatory subset of the management review output.

§8a Abs.3 requires structured documented proof (audit reports, certificates) submitted every two years; ISO 22301 §7.5 requires documented information to be retained as evidence — the regulatory proof package is a subset of required BCMS documented information.
